Analyze Feature Correlation

Author

jmontero

Published

November 2, 2024

Load packages

# Load required packages, install if required
commonly_used_packages = c("data.table", "dplyr", "data.table", "ggplot2", "tidyr")
new_packages = c(
                 "ggpubr",  # Publication-ready plots
                 "Hmisc",   # For p-values and coefficient computation
                 "corrplot",# Visualizations for correlation matrices
                 "ggpubr",  # Publication-ready plots
         "knitr",   # tables
                 "DT",      # Interactive tables in R Markdown and Shiny
                 "forcats",  # Sorting
         "RColorBrewer" # Color scales
                ) # Introduce other packages to import
pkgs = c(commonly_used_packages, new_packages)
for (pkg in pkgs) { 
  if (!suppressMessages(require(pkg, character.only = TRUE, quietly = TRUE))) { 
    install.packages(pkg, character.only = TRUE, repos = "https://cran.uni-muenster.de/", quiet = TRUE) 
    require(pkg, character.only = TRUE, quietly = TRUE) 
  } 
}

Parse argument from command line

dir="/vol/agcpgl/jmontero/features/tidymodels"
file = as.character(paste0(dir, "/", params$file))

Feature importance

Import importance score file

importance = fread(file)
# Cols: Classifier, `Importance Type`, Feature, `Feature name`, Importance and Direction

Edit importance table

# Remove unnecessary columns
importance = importance %>% select(!c(Feature))
# Cols: Classifier, `Importance Type`, `Feature name`
# Rename columns for easier handling
colnames(importance) = c("classifier", "importance_type", "feature_name", "importance", "direction")

Importance table

Comparing feature importance values

Feature importance heatmap

Comparing feature importance rankings

Convert to wide format

# Removing importance col that will no longer be used
importance = importance %>% select(!importance)
# Assign a unique number to each feature
importance_mapping = unique(importance$feature_name) %>%
  data.frame(feature_name = ., feature_number = seq_along(.))
# Substitute features with their corresponding numbers
importance = importance %>%
  left_join(importance_mapping, by = "feature_name") %>%
  select(-feature_name) %>%
  rename(feature_name = feature_number)
# Adding rank column by grouping
importance = importance %>%
  group_by(classifier, importance_type) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(rank = row_number())
# Group classifier and importance type
importance = importance %>% dplyr::mutate(group = paste0(classifier, ", ", importance_type)) %>% ungroup()
# Keep only group, rank and feature_name
importance = importance %>% select(group, rank, feature_name)
# Pivot to wide format
importance_wide = importance %>%
  pivot_wider(
    names_from = group,
    values_from = feature_name
  )
# Convert to matrix
importance_wide = as.matrix(importance_wide)

Calculate Spearman’s rank correlation between feature importances

cor_result = rcorr(importance_wide[, -1], type = "spearman")

Feature Spearman’s ranking correlation heatmap

Feature Spearman’s ranking correlation barplot

## Convert matrix format to data frame with comparison metrics
# Melt the correlation matrix into long format
cor_df = suppressWarnings(melt(cor_result$r, varnames = c("Feature1", "Feature2"), value.name = "Spearman_Cor"))
# Melt the p-value matrix into long format
pval_df = suppressWarnings(melt(cor_result$P, varnames = c("Feature1", "Feature2"), value.name = "P_value"))
# Combine the correlation and p-value dataframes
combined_df = merge(cor_df, pval_df, by = c("Feature1", "Feature2"))
# Remove duplicates and self-comparisons (where Feature1 == Feature2)
tidy_df = combined_df[combined_df$Feature1 != combined_df$Feature2, ]
# Optionally, remove duplicate pairs (e.g., keep only x-y, not y-x)
tidy_df = tidy_df[!duplicated(t(apply(tidy_df[, c("Feature1", "Feature2")], 1, sort))), ]
# Create comparison column
tidy_df = tidy_df %>% dplyr::mutate(Comparison = paste0(Feature1, " - ", Feature2)) %>% select(Feature1, Feature2, Comparison, Spearman_Cor, P_value) %>% arrange(desc(Spearman_Cor))
# Keep only relevant comparisons (only compare classifiers by the same method when possible)
tidy_df = tidy_df %>%
  separate(Feature1, into = c("Classifier1", "Method1"), sep = ", ", remove = FALSE) %>%
  separate(Feature2, into = c("Classifier2", "Method2"), sep = ", ", remove = FALSE)
tidy_df = tidy_df %>%
  dplyr::filter(Classifier1 == Classifier2 | Method1 == Method2 | Classifier1 %in% c("Student t-test", "Logistic Regression") | Classifier2 %in% c("Student t-test", "Logistic Regression"))
tidy_df = tidy_df %>%
  select(Comparison, Spearman_Cor, P_value)
# Add significance marks
tidy_df = tidy_df %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    Significance = case_when(
      P_value < 0.001 ~ "***",
      P_value < 0.01 ~ "**",
      TRUE ~ " ")
    )
# Rename with abbreviations
tidy_df = tidy_df %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    Comparison_short = stringr::str_replace_all(
      Comparison,
      c(
        "Random Forest" = "RF",
        "Decision Tree" = "DT",
        "Logistic Regression" = "LR",
        "Student t-test" = "ST",
        "Impurity-based Importance" = "imp",
        "Permutation-based Importance" = "per",
        "Coefficient-based Importance" = "coe",
        "-log10\\(BH-transformed P-value\\)" = "bhp"
      )
    )
  )
# Reorder Comparison_short by Spearman_Cor in descending order
tidy_df = tidy_df %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    Comparison_short = forcats::fct_reorder(Comparison_short, Spearman_Cor, .desc = TRUE)
  )

Barplot

# Plot
coeff_plot =
  ggplot(tidy_df, aes(x = Comparison_short, y = Spearman_Cor)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "skyblue") +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = Significance),
    vjust = -0.5,
    size = 5
  ) +
  labs(
    title = NULL,  # Remove the plot title
    x = "",
    y = "Spearmans Correlation Coefficient (rho)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
        axis.title.x = element_blank(),
        panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  )
print(coeff_plot)
